name: GEKKO (Dynamic Optimization and Control)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); BYU PRISM Lab (John Hedengren)

auth.notes: Local or APMonitor cloud (no auth for local)

performance.notes: APMonitor solver backend; IPOPT for NLP; handles DAE and optimal control

agent_readiness.notes: Python solver for dynamic optimization, optimal control, and nonlinear MPC. from gekko import GEKKO. m = GEKKO(). x = m.Var(0, lb=-1, ub=1). y = m.Var(0). m.Equation(y == x**2). m.Obj(x + y). m.solve(). IMODE 1-9: steady-state, dynamic, MPC, estimation. Differential-algebraic equations (DAE): m.Equation(x.dt() == -x + u). Real-time optimization. Model predictive control. Process control and chemical engineering. Robotics path optimization. APOPT, IPOPT, BPOPT solvers.

gotchas: Primarily designed for chemical engineering / process control applications
IMODE parameter (1-9) is not intuitive without reading docs
APMonitor cloud fallback can cause unexpected network calls
Smaller community than Pyomo or OR-Tools
best_when: Model predictive control (MPC) and real-time optimization
Dynamic optimization problems with differential equations
avoid_when: Static LP/MIP optimization (OR-Tools or PuLP simpler)
General nonlinear programming without dynamics (Pyomo cleaner)
Teams outside process control / chemical engineering
summary: GEKKO is a Python solver for dynamic optimization and model predictive control. m = GEKKO(); x = m.Var(lb=-1, ub=1); m.Equation(x.dt() == -x + u) for DAE systems. IMODE 6 for MPC. Solvers: APOPT, IPOPT, BPOPT. Real-time optimization. Designed for chemical engineering and process control. MIT license. Smaller community than Pyomo — best for MPC and dynamic optimization problems with differential equations. Use Pyomo for static nonlinear programming.
